file_insights.version_strategy: Pinned major versions with upper bounds for stability

file_insights.architecture: Composition pattern - includes base requirements and extends with dev-only tools

patterns_discovered.benefit: Allows lean production deployments without unnecessary dev tools
patterns_discovered.implementation: Two-file approach with dev file inheriting from base

patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents breaking changes while allowing security updates
Explains purpose of each dependency category
Allows users to install only needed optional components

gotchas_discovered.detail: Uses psycopg with binary flag - assumes PostgreSQL as database backend
gotchas_discovered.potential_problem: Will fail if different database backend is used without modification

gotchas_discovered.detail: celery[redis] makes Redis a required dependency for async tasks
gotchas_discovered.potential_problem: Increases infrastructure requirements; could be optional

gotchas_discovered.issue: Sentry marked optional but in main dev file
gotchas_discovered.detail: sentry-sdk[django] listed in dev requirements with comment '(Optional - Production)'
gotchas_discovered.potential_problem: Inconsistent - truly optional deps should be separate or clearly documented as installable only if needed
Minor version changes could introduce subtle breaking changes in development

gotchas_discovered.detail: No mention of packages like django-model-utils, django-extensions for common utilities
gotchas_discovered.potential_problem: May need additions as project evolves

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created dual requirements files with comprehensive dependency coverage
approach_outcome.completeness: Both production and development environments fully specified
quality_aspects: Clear organization by purpose (production vs development)
Covers REST API, async tasks, caching, and debugging needs
Includes code quality tools (black, ruff, mypy)

recommendations.suggestion: Consider making Celery/Redis optional by creating a requirements-optional.txt
recommendations.rationale: Not all deployments may need async task queue; reduces base dependency footprint

recommendations.suggestion: Add Python version compatibility note in file headers
recommendations.rationale: Currently states 3.10+ but Django 5.0 requires 3.10+; make this explicit
Allows developers to install only relevant toolsets for their workflow

recommendations.suggestion: Consider using pip-tools to lock transitive dependencies

recommendations.rationale: Different deployment targets may need specialized dependencies

recommendations.suggestion: Document the update cadence and strategy for dependencies
recommendations.rationale: Helps team understand when/how to update packages
